title: Caffè Florian, Venezia (Italia)
tags: elsenyordelsbertins canon eos250d efs1855 vacances2023 italia italy veneto venecia venezia venice unesco cafe caffe musica music
description: Caffè Florian és un cafè històric de la ciutat de Venècia, situat sota els pòrtics de la Procuratie Nuove a la plaça de San Marco. És el cafè més antic d'Itàlia i del món. Va ser inaugurat el 29 de desembre de 1720 per Floriano Francesconi amb el nom Alla Venezia Trionfante, però des del primer moment els venecians van dir simplement "andémo da Floriàn", del nom del propietari en dialecte venecià. Des d'aleshores ha continuat el seu negoci diari de cafè ininterrompudament fins als nostres dies, convertint-se en una destinació privilegiada per a venecians, italians i estrangers. Floriano Francesconi va inspirar el personatge de Ridolfo de la Bottega del caffè de Carlo Goldoni. Com altres cafès ubicats a la Piazza San Marco, els clients a l'aire lliure són entretinguts per una petita banda. A més, l'acompanyament musical requereix un suplement a les begudes. Una particularitat són els uniformes del personal del cafè: el xef de rang porta un llaç negre, els dos demichefs són grisos, els comiss són blancs. Els cambrers porten jaquetes blanques al matí, mentre que a la tarda el xef porta frac i la resta d'empleats vesteixen jaquetes negres. Dues orquestres s'alternen al cafè. Pàgina a la UNESCO World Heritage List. A Google Maps.

title: Campanile di San Marco
tags: 2014 venice italy veneto architecture brick cafe carving church city court portal renaissance sculpture spire square statue stone tower weathervane window arch pattern art
description: "It is the tallest structure in Venice and is colloquially termed "el paròn de casa" (the master of the house).  The campanile was initially built as a watch tower to sight approaching ships and protect the entry to the city. It also served as a landmark to guide Venetian ships safely into harbour. Construction began in the early tenth century. The tower was slowly raised in height and acquired a belfry and a spire in the twelfth century. In the fourteenth century the spire was gilded, making the tower visible to distant ships in the Adriatic.  The tower is capped by a pyramidal spire, at the top of which sits a golden weathervane in the form of the archangel Gabriel. The campanile reached its present form in 1514. The current tower was reconstructed in 1912 after the original collapsed in 1902.  It was rebuilt exactly as it was — "dov’era e com’era" ("where it was and how it was")."  Venice. 2014

title: Political Poster
tags: venice italy man poster cafe wheatpaste political it smoking giraffe venezia shoppingbag veneto zattere acquadiparma lostatofalostruzzo
description: Google translate translates this as "The state plays the ostrich", which is at least a bit strange as the picture looks nothing like an ostrich.  A quick check suggests that it refers to a contention that taxes are taken from current workers to pay the pensions of retirees without guaranteeing future pensions for thos current workers.  I'd welcome a better translation or further information.

title: St Mark's Square At Night
tags: plaza venice italy building architecture night table cafe twilight chair italia dusk bluehour venezia piazzasanmarco stmarkssquare
description: I wanted to visit St. Mark's Square before sunrise when it is nearly empty.  However between the jet lag and head cold I picked up on the flight to Venice I couldn't drag myself out of bed that early.  St. Mark's is jammed with people during the day, and is still pretty busy at night, but in a nice way.  Still, I wanted a cleaner photo so I took around 8 shots from this location and did a bit of crowd removal in Photoshop.

title: The Venice of Old? [130/366]
tags: plaza venice italy building tower church architecture cafe cathedral mansion basilicadisanmarco veneto 365photoproject
description: Day 130.  We only had a short time in Venice - one night - and so didn't have time to get much off the beaten path. Thus, on the morning of our second day of vacation we headed for Saint Mark's.   It is hard to take a photo of Piazza San Marco that has not been taken a thousand times already. Additionally, since we were there in the morning and the light was behind the church (and half the front facade was covered in scaffolding), I didn't really focus on the church itself. Instead, I tried to capture something that might represent how San Marco felt before it was overrun by cruise ship daytrippers - elegant cafes overlooking the central meeting place of a rich and aristocractic city. The sepia tone is maybe too much, I don't know.   Three more in comments. Next up, Florence (although a few more Venice shots may make their way to Flickr over the next week or so).  Taken May 9, 2012 in Venice, Veneto, Italy ¹⁄₃₂₀ sec at f/10, ISO100, no flash. Lens: EF-S10-22mm f/3.5-4.5 USM @ 10mm

tags: music night cafe nikon jordan florian venecia d60
description: Caffè Florian is a coffee house situated in the Procuratie Nuove of Piazza San Marco, Venice. It was established in 1720, and is a contender for the title of the oldest coffee house in continuous operation.  Caffè Florian es un café situado en el Nuove Procuratie de la Piazza San Marco, Venecia. Se  estableció  en 1720, y es un aspirante para el título de la cafetería más antigua en funcionamiento continuo.   En el menu del Florian dice:  "Italia es el pais mas hermoso del mundo.  "Venecia es la ciudad mas hermosa de Italia.  "Piazza San Marco es la plaza mas hermosa de Venecia "El Cafe Florian es el mas hermoso de Piazza San Marco. "Por lo tanto usted esta tomando un cafe en el lugar mas hermoso del mundo.

title: Caffè Florian, Venezia
tags: venice italy cafe italian san italia historic marco piazza florian venezia caffè storico
description: Opened in 1720 on Piazza San Marco in Venice, the Caffè Florian is Italy's oldest café. When it opened, it was called "Venezia Trionfante" (Triumphant Venice), but soon became known by its patrons as Florian's (the first owner's name). For its location on the porticoes of the Procuratie nuove, and for its decadent decor, the cafe is today one of the landmarks of the city.

title: Floodlighting
tags: lighting venice italy reflection water night cafe flood piazza venezia sinking sanmarco stmarkssquare
description: It's been 15 years since I was last in Venice and I can safely state that, yes, the city is going under the waves.  I always assumed that when St. Marks Square flooded, the sea came over the edge where the steps meet the lagoon, so I was surprised to see the water bubbling up through the actual stone slabs.  Even at low tide, the water levels are higher than I remember. There is a spot next to the Rialto Bridge where I once took a photo from but is now permenantly under water.  It's a bit scary that in the few decades of my life, I have watched an ancient city disappearing under the sea and glaciers recede faster than my hairline.

title: Floodlighting
tags: lighting venice italy reflection water night cafe flood piazza venezia sinking sanmarco stmarkssquare
description: It's been 15 years since I was last in Venice and I can safely state that, yes, the city is going under the waves.  I always assumed that when St. Marks Square flooded, the sea came over the edge where the steps meet the lagoon, so I was surprised to see the water bubbling up through the actual stone slabs.  Even at low tide, the water levels are higher than I remember. There is a spot next to the Rialto Bridge where I once took a photo from but is now permenantly under water.  It's a bit scary that in the few decades of my life, I have watched an ancient city disappearing under the sea and glaciers recede faster than my hairline.
